_meta: Verdicts rendered by the active Claude session (Option A — no API key). For each case the judge asked: would the COMPRESSED prompt elicit the same answer as the ORIGINAL — was any instruction, constraint, number, negation, ordering, or code/identifier lost? preserved=true means semantically equivalent for answering purposes.

refactor-validate::L1-filler.reason: Only fillers removed (please/just/basically/honestly/really). All load-bearing parts intact: refactor parse_config, validate input is a dict, raise ValueError on missing 'host', keep return type unchanged.

refactor-validate::L2-aggressive.reason: Identical to L1 output; same fillers removed, every instruction + the 'host'/ValueError/return-type constraints preserved.

refactor-validate::L3-learned.reason: Learned tier produced output identical to L2 (no further reduction); intent fully preserved.

scale-deploy::L1-filler.reason: Numbers and negation survive: 5 replicas, 512Mi memory limit, and 'don't touch the CPU request' all intact; only just/simply/please/really removed.

scale-deploy::L2-aggressive.reason: Identical to L1; 5 replicas / 512Mi / don't-touch-CPU all preserved.

scale-deploy::L3-learned.reason: Identical to L2 output; all numbers and the CPU negation preserved.

debug-flaky::L1-filler.reason: Code span `assert 2 == 3` reproduced verbatim; explain-why-fails, suggest-concrete-fix, be-concise all intact; only honestly/just/really/basically/please removed.

debug-flaky::L2-aggressive.reason: Also dropped intensifiers 'obviously'/'clearly'; instruction unchanged (explain why `assert 2 == 3` fails, suggest a concrete fix, be concise). The code span is preserved exactly.

debug-flaky::L3-learned.reason: Identical to L2; code span and the explain/fix/concise instructions preserved.

summarize-constraint::L1-filler.reason: Hard constraints survive: 'exactly 3 bullet points', 'under 15 words', 'don't add any preamble'. 'exactly' is not a filler and was kept.

summarize-constraint::L2-aggressive.reason: Identical to L1; the three numeric/format constraints all preserved.

api-instruction::L1-filler.reason: Endpoint /v1/health, status 200, JSON body {"status":"ok"}, and 'never touches the database' all preserved verbatim; only kindly/simply/please removed.

api-instruction::L2-aggressive.reason: Also dropped 'absolutely' ('make absolutely sure' -> 'make sure'); the constraint 'never touches the database at all' is unchanged, as are the path/status/JSON.

api-instruction::L3-learned.reason: Identical to L2; endpoint, status, JSON body, and DB negation preserved.

ordered-steps::L1-filler.reason: Ordering preserved exactly: generate new -> add alongside old -> revoke old, plus 'keep that exact order'. Only really/just/please removed.

ordered-steps::L2-aggressive.reason: Identical to L1; the three ordered steps and the explicit ordering instruction preserved.

ordered-steps::L3-learned.reason: Identical to L2; step order preserved.

numbers-budget::L1-filler.reason: All numbers and qualifiers survive: timeout 30 seconds, budget cap 500 dollars, 'at most 3 times', exponential backoff, 'don't change any of those numbers'. Only please/just/honestly removed.

numbers-budget::L2-aggressive.reason: Also dropped 'clearly' ('confirm clearly' -> 'confirm'); every number (30s/500/3) + backoff + the don't-change negation intact.

numbers-budget::L3-learned.reason: Identical to L2; all numbers and the negation preserved.

negation-careful::L1-filler.reason: Both critical negations intact: 'do NOT approve ... if there are any failing tests' and 'do not merge it yourself under any circumstances'. Only basically/please/really removed.

negation-careful::L2-aggressive.reason: Also dropped 'obviously'; both negations ('do NOT approve' on failing tests, 'do not merge yourself') remain fully intact — meaning unchanged.

negation-careful::L3-learned.reason: Identical to L2; both negations preserved.
